hawkish_phrases: services inflation had remained unchanged at 4.0% in March
domestic inflation remained high...year-on-year domestic inflation and services inflation were edging down only slowly
further confirmation was required of weakening pressures on both wages and profits
Upside risks to inflation included the heightened geopolitical tensions, especially in the Middle East, which could push energy prices and freight costs higher
dovish_phrases: it would be appropriate to reduce the current level of monetary policy restriction
It was seen as plausible that the Governing Council would be in a position to start easing monetary policy restriction at the June meeting
A few members felt sufficiently confident that the three elements of the Governing Council's reaction function gave grounds for a reduction in the policy rates already at the present meeting
peak restrictiveness in euro area financing conditions might have passed
The risk of undershooting the inflation target and eventually having to pay too high a price in terms of declining activity was now seen as being at least as high as the risk of acting too early
summary: The ECB held rates unchanged with near-unanimous support, but signaled it would likely be appropriate to reduce restriction at the June meeting if data confirmed the March projections, with a few members already favoring a cut at this meeting.
